mnemonics: P.R.A.C.T.I.C.E.S. - Principles Require Action, Careful Translation Into Concrete Ethical Systems
keyPoints: Translation requires interdisciplinary methods combining ethics, computer science, law, and social science.
A key research approach is to develop and validate measurable metrics for abstract ethical principles.
Implementation involves creating technical standards, governance frameworks, and auditing procedures.
commonMistakes: Assuming ethical principles can be directly coded without interpretation or contextual adaptation.
Focusing solely on technical solutions while neglecting the necessary policy, legal, and organizational structures.
Failing to involve diverse stakeholders (e.g., impacted communities, ethicists, policymakers) in the translation process.
